summary: First-finish smoke test for BrandKrt Part 1A. Backend health, admin login, register validations, duplicate-email guard, logout cookie-clear, forgot-password (no enumeration), contact endpoint, profile cookie session, and refresh all PASS. Landing page renders all sections; theme toggle flips html.dark; protected route /profile redirects to /login; admin login + post-login user menu work. Two backend issues block full email flows: verify-email and reset-password both return 500 due to a naive/aware datetime comparison bug. Brute-force lockout does not fire on the public URL because identifier uses request.client.host which varies across proxy upstream IPs (e.g. 10.219.2.4 vs 10.219.2.7), splitting the failure counter.
passed: POST /api/auth/login admin -> 200, sets access_token + refresh_token cookies
POST /api/auth/register valid influencer -> 200, sets cookies, returns user
POST /api/auth/register role=admin -> 400 'Admin accounts cannot be self-registered'
POST /api/auth/logout -> 200, Set-Cookie Max-Age=0 for both tokens
Email tokens (verify + reset) logged to backend stdout as expected (EMAIL_PROVIDER=console)
Frontend / landing page renders navbar + Features/Pricing/FAQ/Contact sections
Admin login on /login redirects to /profile and navbar-user-menu appears

failed.issue: Returns 500 Internal Server Error for any valid token. Endpoint completely broken.
failed.evidence: server.py:355  TypeError: can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes (rec['expires_at'] from Mongo is naive UTC, compared against datetime.now(timezone.utc)).

failed.issue: Returns 500 Internal Server Error for any valid token. Password reset flow completely broken; subsequent login with new password fails (401).
failed.evidence: server.py:340 same TypeError: can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes. Captured in /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log.

failed.issue: 6th and 7th consecutive failed logins still return 401, never 429. Lockout does not engage end-to-end via the public URL.
failed.evidence: Mongo db.login_attempts shows the same email being counted under two different identifiers because request.client.host reports the proxy upstream IP (observed 10.219.2.4 and 10.219.2.7), splitting the counter below LOCKOUT_THRESHOLD. Also, once locked_until IS set, _is_locked will hit the same naive/aware datetime TypeError on the next attempt.

failed.issue: JWT access_token is not invalidated server-side after logout. Reusing a previously-issued access_token cookie still authenticates /api/auth/me with 200.
failed.evidence: Curl with the cookie value (not the cookie jar that the server cleared) still returns the user for /api/auth/me after POST /api/auth/logout. Acceptable for stateless JWT MVP, but worth noting since the spec asks that 'subsequent GET /api/auth/me returns 401' — in a browser this passes because the cookie is cleared; via raw token replay it does not.

action_items: Fix datetime tz mismatch in server.py for verify_email (line 355), reset_password (line 340), and _is_locked (line 204). Normalize by storing as aware UTC and comparing with datetime.now(timezone.utc), e.g. wrap reads: 'exp = rec[\'expires_at\']; if exp.tzinfo is None: exp = exp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)'. Same fix needed wherever Mongo datetimes are compared.
Re-test verify-email (single-use + reuse 400), reset-password (and follow-up login with new password) after the tz fix.
Make brute-force identifier resilient to proxies: read X-Forwarded-For (left-most) or use only the email as the identifier, then re-test 5 failed attempts -> 429 on the 6th.
(Optional / out of scope for Part 1A) Add a token/jti denylist or short access-token TTL + refresh rotation to fully invalidate access_token on logout.
